title: Privacy Impact Assessment addendum for Temporary Border Measures under the Quarantine Act (September 30, 2022, and March 24, 2023) – Roxham Road - Canada.ca
content:
## Privacy Impact Assessment addendum for Temporary Border Measures under the Quarantine Act (September 30, 2022, and March 24, 2023) – Roxham Road
The Temporary Border Measures under the Quarantine Act – Roxham Road was implemented to manage the influx of irregular asylum seekers entering Canada through the Roxham Road crossing. The program involved on-site health assessments and coordination with local health authorities to prevent the spread of communicable diseases. The program was delivered by the Public Health Agency of Canada in collaboration with the Royal Canadian Mounted Police, Canada Border Services Agency, and Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada. It was managed through direct federal involvement and coordination with provincial health authorities. The program was delivered by the Public Health Agency of Canada in collaboration with the Royal Canadian Mounted Police, Canada Border Services Agency, and Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada). It was managed through direct federal involvement and coordination with provincial health authorities

## Why a Privacy Impact Assessment was completed
A Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) addendum was completed as the border measures that were put in place at Roxham Road were a substantial modification to the existing Quarantine Program where personal information was being used to determine if an individual was required to quarantine for a period upon entry to Canada.

## Additional information
This PIA focused on the Public Health Agency of Canada's collection, use, disclosure, and retention and disposition of personal information involved in the business processes for the Roxham Road Temporary Border Measures. It did not include the processes for disclosing information to local/provincial health authorities in the event an individual needed medical attention, as these processes were not finalized before the cessation of activities at Roxham Road.
The PIA recommended mitigation actions in the following risk areas:
- Control and disclosure of records: Ensuring that any applications used in future quarantine activities are authorized for Protected A or B information.
- Sharing of information within Health Canada: Implementing an audit log or other access controls for Microsoft Forms and Microsoft List (or other applications) if they are used in future quarantine activities.
- Retention and disposition authorities: Establishing the retention period for personal information stored in the Quarantine Case Management System and determining the authority for the Quarantine Program.
- Maintaining accuracy: Ensuring that privacy notices would be updated to reflect changes to the processes in the future for quarantine activities.

## Related Personal Information Bank
Info  Source describes how PHAC manages your personal  information. Refer to the Personal Information Bank PHAC  PPU 074 - Pandemic Border Measures .

## For more information about this Privacy Impact Assessment
If you would like more information about this PIA addendum, please contact: tpp-ppv@phac-aspc.gc.ca .
